summary.intent: Fix a branch coverage CI failure on PR #704 (range-calendar-core, issue #287) where ars-components dropped below the 90% branch coverage threshold after review-fix commits.
summary.outcome: Coverage raised from 89.8% to 90.4% branch coverage by adding targeted RangeCalendar tests covering uncovered branches (readonly/hover paths, PartialEq short-circuit, disabled attribute branches, keyboard navigation). Commit pushed and CI re-triggered.

repo: CI measures coverage on the PR merge commit (branch + main), not the branch head alone — a thin local margin can become a miss after merging with main changes.

cargo xcov / cargo xci-fast run the workspace-level native coverage gate; running a targeted `cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-components --lib --text -- <module>` is NOT equivalent and will not catch threshold failures.
ars-components requires ≥90% branch coverage; the codecov gate has two checks: codecov/project (workspace total) and codecov/patch (new code delta) — they can fail independently.
@codex review is triggered via a GitHub issue comment on the PR, and signals activity with an eyes reaction before posting its review.
cargo +nightly llvm-cov test -p ars-components --lib --branch --text produces a line-annotated coverage report useful for hunting specific uncovered branches.

code.finding: Multiple uncovered branches identified: readonly/no-anchor hover paths, no-op HoverEnd, direct navigation events, offset-specific cell attrs, nav disabled branches with min/max, range announcement message closures, and PartialEq short-circuit paths.

Test file grew from ~140 lines to 44 tests across multiple rounds of coverage fixes. Two separate Messages::default() values are not equality-comparable (callbacks are identity-sensitive); must clone the same instance to test message equality.

workflow: After review-fix commits that touch coverage-sensitive code, always run cargo xcov or xci-fast rather than a targeted per-module llvm-cov slice — the threshold gate is workspace-level.
Maintain a meaningful margin (>0.3%) over coverage thresholds to absorb merge-commit drift from main.

When CI coverage fails, pull job logs with `gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/<id>/logs | rg ...` to separate workspace threshold failures from Codecov patch gate failures before deciding the fix strategy.
Polling CI with `gh pr checks` and `gh run view --json status,conclusion,jobs` is more reliable than waiting for PR page updates; coverage and release verification are the longest jobs.

friction: CI measures the merge commit rather than the branch head — a 90.1% local result failed remotely because main had moved, requiring multiple additional rounds of coverage work.
Initial approach only ran targeted module coverage instead of the full cargo xcov gate, which masked the threshold problem until CI caught it.

Three separate rounds of test additions were needed before the branch percentage moved enough (90.1% → 90.1% → 90.4%) because line coverage gains don't always translate to branch counter improvements.
Coverage CI job is slow (took ~10+ minutes), requiring many polling intervals before getting a result.
One added test assertion incorrectly assumed two separately-constructed Messages::default() values are equal; identity-sensitive callbacks caused a test failure that required a fix.
open_items: Coverage margin at 90.4% is still relatively thin — future changes to ars-components that touch branch-heavy code risk dropping below threshold again.

The root cause of why xci-fast passed on the initial PR push but coverage failed post-review-fix was not fully diagnosed — unclear if xci-fast was run at all after the follow-up commits or just skipped.
Framework-facing connect helpers in range_calendar/mod.rs still have some uncovered branches that were intentionally left for a future pass.
